meaning: v.鍐嶆鎸囨淳,閲嶆柊鎸囧畾
examples: He Hua's village committee is just about to redesignate itself as the he Hua village Economic Shareholding Co-operative, a process that has taken four years.
Planning is the responsibility of local government, and, as Mr Osborne pointed out, councils already have the power to redesignate land for building.
Employees are starting to act on a new law that allows you to redesignate money stashed in your traditional 401(k) into Roth money, keeping the money in your existing 401(k).
